name: Generator using yield
question_text: What does a function that contains a yield statement become in Python?
general_feedback: Any function containing yield is compiled into a generator function. Calling it does not run the body — it returns a generator object that produces values lazily as it is iterated (via next or a for-loop).
correct_feedback: Correct — yield turns the function into a lazy iterator factory.

answers.text: A generator function whose call returns a generator object.
answers.feedback: yield turns the function into a lazy iterator factory.

answers.text: A coroutine that is scheduled on the event loop.
answers.feedback: Coroutines are declared with async def; yield alone does not create one.

answers.text: A regular function that returns the first yielded value.
answers.feedback: Calling a generator function does not execute the body; it returns a generator.

answers.text: A syntax error, because yield is only valid in async code.
answers.feedback: yield is valid in ordinary functions and creates a generator.
